query: Tokyo population 2024 official data Japan statistics
answer: The metro area population of Tokyo in 2024 was 37,115,000, a 0.21% decline from 2023. The Tokyo metropolitan government estimates the population will continue to decline. The prefecture population was 14,002,534 in 2024.

results.title: Japan’s Population Falls Again in 2024 | Nippon.com
results.snippet: [Latest](/en/latest/)[In-depth](/en/in-depth/)[Japan Data](/en/japan-data/)[Guide](/en/guide-to-japan/)[Video/Live](/en/japan-video/)[Japanese](https://japanese.nippon.com/)[Japan Glances](/en/japan-glances/)[Images](/en/japan-images/)[People](/en/people/)[Blog](/en/blog/). * [Japan Data](/en/japan-data/). * [Guide](/en/guide-to-japan/). * [Japan Data](/en/japan-data/). [Japan Data](/en/series/japan-data/). Japan’s population was 124,330,690, as of January 1, 2025, after a 0.44% year-on-year decrease, according to a demographic survey published by the Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications. While the Japanese population in Tokyo rose by 16,825 or 0.13%, it fell in all 46 other prefectures. The largest decreases were mainly in the north of Tōhoku: 1.91% in [Akita](https://www.nippon.com/en/guide-to-japan/pref05/), 1.72% in [Aomori](https://www.nippon.com/en/guide-to-japan/pref02/), and 1.69% in [Iwate](https://www.nippon.com/en/guide-to-japan/pref03/). There was also a 1.71% drop in the Shikoku prefecture of [Kōchi](https://www.nippon.com/en/guide-to-japan/pref39/). The prefecture with the highest population (including Japanese and non-Japanese) was [Tokyo](https://www.nippon.com/en/guide-to-japan/pref13/) with 14,002,534 residents, followed by [Kanagawa](https://www.nippon.com/en/guide-to-japan/pref14/) at 9,202,559, and [Osaka](https://www.nippon.com/en/guide-to-japan/pref27/) at 8,771,961. The least populated prefecture was [Tottori](https://www.nippon.com/en/guide-to-japan/pref31/), with just 534,003 residents. The total population of the three major metropolitan areas centered on Tokyo, Osaka, and Nagoya was 66,008,727, declining for the fifth consecutive year, but still representing 53.1% of the total population. ### Prefectures with the Highest and Lowest Populations (Non-Japanese).
